qa_pairs.question: What should a user do if Ansys Fluent crashes during the initial auto mesh partitioning phase on an Alliance cluster like Narval when using standard Intel-based scripts?
qa_pairs.answer: If Ansys Fluent crashes during the initial auto mesh partitioning phase with standard Intel-based scripts on an Alliance cluster like Narval, users can either utilize the more robust OpenMPI-based scripts provided for Narval, or manually perform the mesh partitioning within the Fluent graphical user interface (GUI) to inspect partition statistics and specify the method before resubmitting the job with Intel scripts.

qa_pairs.question: What are the recommended guidelines for configuring mesh partitions for optimal efficiency in Ansys Fluent simulations on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: For optimal efficiency in Ansys Fluent simulations on Alliance clusters, the number of mesh partitions should be an integral multiple of the number of cores used. Additionally, it is recommended to ensure there are at least 10,000 cells per core.
